race.name: Tour of Nilgiris
race.tagline: Asia's premier multi-stage endurance cycling expedition through the Western Ghats

climate.description: December in the Nilgiris typically features cool mountain conditions with potential mist and rain on higher elevations. Temperatures generally range from cool to mild across the three-state region.

features: The tour traverses the Western Ghats, one of the world's biodiversity hotspots, featuring lush forests, tea plantations, winding mountain roads, and high-altitude sections
Riders encounter a mix of rolling terrain, steep climbs, and technical descents across varied landscapes

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 81. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 77.

course_description.character: An immersive, multi-stage endurance expedition through the Western Ghats, blending competitive climbing challenges with cultural and environmental immersion. The route emphasizes sustained elevation gain, technical descents, and high-altitude terrain across three states, with tea plantations, forest corridors, and iconic mountain vistas creating a transformative cycling experience beyond traditional racing.
course_description.signature_challenge: Kalahatti's 12 km, 36-hairpin climb represents the tour's defining suffering zone and most iconic challenge. The ascent tests climbing strength, pacing discipline, and mental fortitude while delivering one of Asia's most memorable cycling experiences.
suffering_zones: 12 km ascent with 36 hairpins; iconic, relentless climbing challenge with significant gradient and technical turns. Signature suffering point of the tour.
Multiple ascents approaching and within Ooty region; sustained elevation gain at altitude with rolling, technical terrain.
Extended riding at elevation across Nilgiris plateau; thin air, sustained effort, and recovery demands compound fatigue.
7-day format demands endurance management, pacing strategy, and mental resilience across consecutive high-elevation stages.

logistics.airport: BLR (Bengaluru International Airport), ~60 km from start areakm; CJB (Coimbatore International Airport), ~80 km from Ootykm; MYQ (Mysuru Airport), ~50 km from Mysore stagekm
logistics.lodging_strategy: Fully provided by event organizer; riders stay in arranged accommodations throughout the 7-day tour. Specific hotel details available upon registration.
logistics.transport: Tour of Nilgiris is a fully supported cycling experience managed by Breakaway Sports Unlimited Pvt. Ltd. All logistics—transport, accommodation, nutrition, bike maintenance, medical support, and volunteer coordination—are handled by the organizer. Riders need only arrive and pedal.

history.origin_story: Tour of Nilgiris was founded in 2008 as a grassroots multi-day cycling expedition in the Bangalore cycling community. It has grown from a small group ride with 20-30 participants into one of Asia's most respected endurance cycling events, now in its 15th edition (as of 2025). The event is known for promoting cycling culture, environmental awareness, and the camaraderie of long-distance riding through the Western Ghats biodiversity hotspot.

notable_moments.description: Genesis of Tour of Nilgiris; small group ride with ~20-30 participants, basic support, memorable incidents including riders getting lost and bitten.
Venky completed TFN on a fixed-gear bike, demonstrating the event's accessibility to diverse riding styles and philosophies.
Arun joined as guide and advisor, providing critical feedback that shaped event structure and operations significantly.
Event celebrated 15 years of passion, adventure, and community; established as Asia's benchmark for multi-stage amateur cycling tours.
12 km climb with 36 hairpins; iconic challenge attracting repeat riders. Regular participant Anand summited Kalahatti 7 times across editions.

biased_opinion.summary: Tour of Nilgiris represents the gold standard for multi-stage amateur cycling tours in Asia. The combination of world-class climbing, immersive Western Ghats scenery, and fully supported logistics creates an experience that transcends typical racing. The event's 18-year evolution from a small group ride to a 15,000+ meter, 700+ kilometer expedition demonstrates authentic growth rooted in cycling community values. For endurance-focused riders seeking both competitive challenge and transformative adventure, TFN is non-negotiable.

final_verdict.one_liner: Tour of Nilgiris is Asia's premier multi-stage endurance cycling expedition, combining world-class climbing, biodiversity immersion, and fully supported logistics into a transformative 7-day adventure.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Launched in 2008, Tour of Nilgiris has evolved into one of Asia's most respected endurance cycling events, attracting competitive cyclists and adventure riders from across India and internationally. The 700+ km, 15,000+ meter, 7-day format traverses the Western Ghats across three states, featuring iconic climbs like Kalahatti (12 km, 36 hairpins) and Ooty ascents. The event's fully supported model—covering accommodation, nutrition, bike maintenance, medical aid, and volunteer support—eliminates logistical friction while maintaining significant athletic challenge. With training programs, bike packing services, and a half-tour option (3 days, 300+ km, 7,000+ m), TFN accommodates riders across experience levels while preserving the core endurance ethos. The December timing, tea plantation corridors, and commitment to environmental awareness create an experience that extends beyond racing into cultural immersion. For riders seeking demanding multi-stage cycling with professional support and transformative scenery, Tour of Nilgiris represents exceptional value and authentic community.

videos.title: Rare flower which blooms once every 12 years now in flower
videos.description:
(1 Oct 2006) 
Munnar - 8 September 2006
1. Wide of mountains, tea garden in foreground 
2. Tea plantations with a blanket of thick tea bushes 
3. Tracking shot along mountains
4.  Mountain goat the 'Niligiri Tahr' amid rare flower Neelakurinji (Strobilanthes kunthiana)
5. Various of rare flower
6. Tracking shot over flower blooms
7. Wide of mountain slope with rare flower blooming
Thiruvananthapuram - 9 September 2006
8. SOUNDBITE (English) Professor Nair, Department of Botany at University of Kerala :
"This is really a legendary plant, that it flowers only once in 12 years.  And the beauty of it is that it has established into a big population and all of them flower together - it is a synchronised flowering. So that makes the beauty on the slopes of the hills especially in Eravikulam National Park , Palni hills and all."
Munnar - 8 September 2006
9. Sign for Eravikulam National Park
10. Flower in bloom inside park , mountains in the background
11. Pan of crowds and queues of visitors waiting to enter  Eravikulam to see the rare flower in bloom
12. Various of tourists
13. Rare mountain goat in park
14. Tourists walking through park looking at flowers
15. Various of rare flowers
16. SOUNDBITE (English) Mr Singh, tourist :
"It is a beautiful sight with beautiful weather with beautiful valleys and with mountains - I don't think there is anywhere in India this kind of sight is seen. It is a beautiful experience."
17. Various of workers plucking leaves in tea garden
18. Woman emptying bag with collected tea leaves onto a sheet
19. Close up of tea leaves
20. Wide of mist rolling in over expanse of rare flower 
Thiruvananthapuram - 9 September 2006
21. SOUNDBITE (English) Professor Nair, Department of Botany at University of Kerala :
"It is an amazing thing which never occurs anywhere in the world. And as you know that it is an endemic (confined to one place) species, so it is present only in certain narrow pockets. Though the population has dwindled, mainly because of the encroachment, especially the plantations, the tea plantations. The Britishers (referring to the former British colonial presence in India) have started destroying the (flower) population so it is very important and need of the hour that we conserve this very unique species,  which has got this this very valuable kind of flowering and it is a kind of instinct for survival for the plant itself."
Munnar - 8 September 2006
22. Close up of rare flower 
23. Close up of honey bee on flower
24. Flowers
25. SOUNDBITE (English) Roy Thomas, wildlife warden:
"We are trying to conserve Neelakurinji in its habitat - that's the most important thing we want to emphasise here, the conservation of Neelakurinji.  During the last two blooms there was heavy destruction in the plant. So this year we have taken steps to control the destruction of Neelakurinji plant."
26. Various of rare flower
27. Wide of flower in bloom on mountain
LEAD IN: 
The flower species known as Neelakurinji only flowers once every twelve years.
2006 is one of those years and from August to October the hills of the Western Ghats are carpeted in purple flowers.
STORYLINE :
The Western Ghats, the lofty mountain ranges in southern India, are witnessing one of the most eagerly awaited phenomena for nature lovers - the spectacular blooming of the Neelakurinji flower.  
Every 12 years, Kerala's famed tea plantation region plays host to the Neelakurinji (botanical name: Strobilanthes kunthiana), a species of flowering shrub that blooms in the high altitudes.
'Neela' means blue in the Malayalam language and 'Kurinji' is the local name of the flower.

The transcripts appear to cover topics unrelated to the Tour of Nilgiris cycling race, including botanical content about flowering plants, content in Tamil language, and brief mentions of heat. No actionable intelligence about course conditions, terrain, challenges, gear recommendations, or race-specific information could be extracted from these video sources.
